source.title: FY2025 GMV Growth Decomposition and 2026 Forecast

extractions.claim: FY2025 GMV growth was ~22-25% YoY, driven primarily by Temu volume rather than domestic ARPU
extractions.evidence: ECDB: 2024 GMV $655B -> 2025 GMV $817B = +24.7% growth. Decomposition: (A) Domestic Pinduoduo: 2024 est. RMB 4.5-5.0T -> 2025 est. RMB 4.5-5.2T = ~5-15% domestic growth. Goldman Sachs noted domestic GMV growth dropped below 20% in mid-2024, and revenue growth decelerated to 10% in FY2025 (from 90% in FY2024). Domestic growth was constrained by: consumer spending weakness, intense competition from Douyin/Taobao, and 100B merchant subsidy program dampening monetization. (B) Temu international: 2024 est. $70.8B -> 2025 est. $92.5B = +31% growth. Temu growth was ~70%+ in H1 plan but dragged down by U.S. tariff shock to ~31% actual. CONCLUSION: Temu contributed most of the incremental GMV dollars despite slower-than-expected growth. Domestic Pinduoduo grew modestly.

verification.verifier_notes: The domestic vs international split is the biggest estimation challenge since PDD does not disclose segment GMV. The conclusion that Temu drove most incremental GMV is robust: domestic revenue growth (OMS as proxy) slowed to +10% avg, while Temu-heavy TS grew faster in absolute RMB terms.

extractions.claim: Domestic ARPU growth stalled in 2025 as PDD reinvested via 100B merchant subsidy program
extractions.evidence: PDD launched '100 billion core program' for merchant support in 2024, continuing into 2025. OMS revenue growth decelerated: Q1 +15%, Q2 +13%, Q3 +8%, Q4 +5%. This sequential deceleration in advertising revenue suggests PDD deliberately restrained monetization pressure on merchants. Marketing expenses surged 44% YoY in Q1 2025. Result: domestic take rate likely flat to slightly down vs 2024, meaning domestic GMV growth roughly tracked domestic OMS revenue growth (~10%).

verification.verifier_notes: OMS deceleration is a hard fact from quarterly earnings. The interpretation (deliberate restraint) is supported by management commentary about ecosystem investment. Alternative explanation: organic advertising demand softening due to macro weakness.

extractions.claim: 2026 GMV forecast: $950B-$1.05T total (domestic $700-780B, Temu $120-130B, other intl $30-40B)
extractions.evidence: DOMESTIC 2026: Modest 5-10% growth from consumer stimulus, quality upgrade initiative. RMB 4.8-5.7T ($670-790B). Central: ~$730B. TEMU 2026: 30% growth target (internal) from $92.5B = $120B base case. EU de minimis removal risk could clip to $110B. US stabilization with semi-managed model. Central: ~$120B. TARIFF SCENARIOS: (A) Base case (no new tariffs): Total $950B. (B) EU de minimis removal mid-2026: -$10-15B from Temu Europe = Total $935-940B. (C) US tariff escalation: -$15-20B from Temu US = Total $930-935B. (D) Both EU and US adverse: Total $900-920B. GROWTH: Base case $950B / $800B 2025 = +19% YoY. Adverse case $910B / $800B = +14% YoY.

verification.verifier_notes: 2026 forecast is inherently uncertain. Key variables: (1) China consumer spending trajectory, (2) EU de minimis policy timing, (3) US tariff regime stability. The range of $900B-$1.05T reflects these scenarios. Growth is decelerating from ~25% (2025) to ~14-19% (2026).
